heroHeadline: 1.5 Million Years of Human Presence in the Southern Levant
heroSubtitle: From Acheulean hominids at Ubeidiya to the Oslo Accords — the world's most intensely documented human habitation, spanning prehistory, the Biblical age, Hellenistic and Roman eras, Islamic and Crusader periods, Ottoman rule, and the modern State of Israel.
footerNote: Data sourced from peer-reviewed archaeology (IAA, Hebrew University, Tel Aviv University), UNESCO, British Museum, and major academic institutions. Contested historical interpretations are presented with multiple scholarly perspectives.
